title: calamagrostis neglecta subsp. borealis
intro: Calamagrostis neglecta subsp. borealis, also known as boreal fescue grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to the boreal regions of North America. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of boreal fescue grass include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of boreal fescue grass can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
